title: Server middleware dependencies are missing from the package manifest

evidence.quote: const { body, param, query, validationResult } = require('express-validator');

reasoning: The owned middleware imports jsonwebtoken, dotenv, express-rate-limit, and express-validator, but none are declared in dependencies or devDependencies. A clean install from this package manifest will not install those modules, so any runtime path that loads these middleware files fails with module resolution errors.
reproduction: On a clean checkout, run `npm install` followed by loading any of these middleware modules; module resolution will fail for the first undeclared package import encountered.
recommendation: Add the middleware runtime packages to dependencies, or remove these server middleware files from this package if they are not part of the shipped application. If they live in a separate backend package, move them there with that package's manifest.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests are included, and there is no package test script. Existing commands shown only include lint, which does not prove runtime dependencies are installed and resolvable.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add an install/load smoke test in CI that runs after `npm ci` and imports the middleware entrypoint(s) from a clean dependency tree.
minimumFixScope: Declare jsonwebtoken, dotenv, express-rate-limit, and express-validator in package.json dependencies, or relocate/remove the middleware from this package.

history.reasoning: The original evidence still exists at the cited root paths: src/middleware/auth.js still requires jsonwebtoken and dotenv, src/middleware/rateLimiter.js still requires express-rate-limit, and src/middleware/validate.js still requires express-validator. The root package.json dependencies/devDependencies still do not declare any of those packages. Current code also still wires the root server tree through src/index.js, which requires ./middleware/rateLimiter and routes that require ./middleware/auth and ./middleware/validate, so a clean install from the root manifest would still be missing these runtime modules. There is now a separate server/package.json that declares these dependencies for server/src, but that does not fix the still-present root src server files against the root package manifest. npm lint and inline module-resolution smoke checks were blocked by execution policy, so the conclusion is based on current source and manifest evidence.
commands: npm run lint (blocked by policy at repository root)
npm run lint (blocked by policy in server/)
